name: Escargots En Cercueils ( Snails in Puff Pastry Shells )
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Chardonnay or dry white wine
3 tbsp Milk (to blend with egg yolk)
recipeInstructions:
Note: Butter should be cut into 12 pieces.
1. Drain snail juices into a small saucepan.
2. Place snails in a bowl.
3. Add wine, onion, shallot, garlic, salt, pepper, and nutmeg to the juices and bring to a boil.
4. Pour marinade over snails. Let cool. Cover and refrigerate for 24 hours.
5. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
6. Butter baking sheets.
7. Roll dough out on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of 1/4".
8. Cut out 36 circles using a 2-inch floured cutter. Arrange the circles on prepared sheets.
9. Using a knife tip, trace lids on each circle, 3/4 inch from the edge; do not cut through the dough.
10. Brush with glaze.
11. Bake pastry until puffed and golden brown, about 10 minutes. Cool slightly on a rack.
12. Using a sharp knife, cut out lids. Discard any soft dough inside shells.
13. Reduce oven temperature to 375°F (190°C).
14. Strain marinade into a large saucepan, pressing down on vegetables to extract all liquid until reduced to 1/2 cup.
15. Remove pan from heat and whisk in 2 pieces of butter.
16. Set pan over low heat and whisk in the remaining butter one piece at a time.
17. Mix in snails, hazelnuts, and parsley.
18. Adjust seasoning.
19. Place 1 snail and some sauce inside each shell. Top with lid.
20. Bake until heated through. Serve immediately.
